---
title: "UIAO Modernization Master Plan — 90-Day Execution Plan"
subtitle: "Sequenced execution against the ratified Phase 0 / Phase 1 / Boundary / Compliance baselines"
doc-type: master-plan
phase: "Phase 3 — Execution (Days 1–90)"
audience: "CIO · CISO · ISSO · Authorizing Official (AO) · 3PAO · Program Management · Engineering Leads"
derived-from: "compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd"
upstream:
  - "phase-0-planning.qmd §6 Deliverables"
  - "phase-0-planning.qmd §7 Risks & Dependencies"
  - "phase-1-architecture.qmd §2 Plane Model"
  - "phase-1-architecture.qmd §7 Conformance Criteria"
  - "boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd §5 Sequencing Constraints"
  - "boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd §6 Conformance Criteria"
  - "compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd §5 Continuous ATO Operating Model"
  - "compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd §7 Conformance Criteria"
date: 2026-04-26
---

# UIAO Modernization Master Plan — 90-Day Execution Plan {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Series Map — you are reading **document 5 of 5**
[Series index](index.qmd) ·
[Phase 0 Planning](phase-0-planning.qmd) →
[Phase 1 Architecture](phase-1-architecture.qmd) →
[Boundary Modernization Appendix](boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd) →
[Compliance & ATO Alignment](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd) →
**90-Day Execution Plan** (this document)
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Document Purpose
This document sequences the first ninety days of UIAO modernization
execution against the ratified Phase 0 Planning Document, Phase 1
Architecture Document, Boundary Modernization Appendix, and Compliance
& ATO Alignment. It defines three thirty-day windows, the objectives
and conformance gates for each, the cross-cutting workstreams that span
all three windows, and the exit criteria that close the 90-day program.
:::

---

## 1. Sequencing Principles

The 90-day execution is governed by four sequencing principles, each
derived from an upstream document.

| # | Principle | Source |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Substrate before Authorization** — Substrate-Tier planes (Identity, Device, Server, Routing) stand up before Authorization-Tier work begins | [Phase 1 §2.2](phase-1-architecture.qmd#plane-layering) |
| 2 | **Boundary before Enforcement** — Boundary classes are inventoried and capture (SRF L4) is in place before any enforcement claim is treated as conformant | [Boundary Appendix §5](boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd#sequencing-constraints-on-downstream-planes) |
| 3 | **Evidence before KSI** — The evidence chain satisfies its six properties before any Tier-1 KSI is exposed to the AO surface | [Compliance §3.1](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#ksi-derivation-rule), [§4.1](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#chain-properties) |
| 4 | **Conformance before cATO** — A deployment must reach evidence-conforming state before authorization-state derivation is accepted by the AO surface | [Compliance §5.1](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#mechanism-1-authorization-state-derivation), [§7](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#conformance-criteria) |

Schedule pressure does not relax these principles; if a window cannot
satisfy them, the window slips, the sequence does not.

---

## 2. Window Structure

The 90-day program is divided into three thirty-day windows. Each window
has a theme, a deliverable set, a conformance gate at its close, and an
explicit dependency on the prior window.

| Window | Theme | Conformance Gate at Close |
|---|---|---|
| **Days 1–30 — Substrate & Discovery** | Stand up substrate planes; discover and inventory boundaries | Substrate-conforming (Phase 1 §7 criteria 1–4) |
| **Days 31–60 — Capture & Binding** | Establish evidence capture (SRF L4) at every boundary; bind controls to planes | Evidence-conforming (Compliance §7 criteria 1–4) |
| **Days 61–90 — Conformance & cATO Posture** | Close the conformance criteria; stand up the cATO authorization-state derivation | Authorization-conforming (Compliance §7 criteria 5–8); cATO posture established |

---

## 3. Days 1–30 — Substrate & Discovery

### 3.1 Objectives

1. Establish named owners for all ten planes.
2. Stand up the Substrate-Tier planes (Identity, Device, Server, Routing) to a working state with canonical interfaces.
3. Complete the boundary class inventory across all six classes (Boundary Appendix §2).
4. Stand up the canonical control library (NIST 800-53 Rev 5, FedRAMP Rev 5, SCuBA, KSI definitions).

### 3.2 Activities

| # | Activity | Output |
|---|---|---|
| A1 | Plane-owner attestation collected for each of the ten planes | Plane registry |
| A2 | Identity plane brought to canonical state with `identity-assertion` interface live | Identity-assertion contract |
| A3 | Device plane brought to canonical state with `posture-report` interface live | Posture-report contract |
| A4 | Server plane brought to canonical state with workload-identity interface live | Workload-identity contract |
| A5 | Routing plane brought to canonical state with `path-state` interface live | Path-state contract |
| A6 | Boundary discovery scan run against all six boundary classes | Boundary registry |
| A7 | Catalog ingestion (Compliance §2.1 step 1) executed for each canonical source | Canonical control library populated |
| A8 | Initial Tier-3 KSI surface stood up against substrate state | Operator-tier KSI feed |

### 3.3 Conformance Gate (Day 30)

The window closes when Phase 1 §7 conformance criteria 1–4 are
demonstrably satisfied:

- All ten planes present with attributed owners.
- Each plane exposes its canonical interfaces with fixed schemas.
- Four-tier layering is enforced.
- Every primary signal class is *carriable* end-to-end (capture in the
  next window will demonstrate it).

If the gate is not satisfied, Days 31–60 do not begin; the window
extends and downstream windows shift. Sequencing principle 1 is non-
negotiable.

---

## 4. Days 31–60 — Capture & Binding

### 4.1 Objectives

1. Establish unconditional evidence capture (SRF L4) at every crossing
   in the boundary registry.
2. Bind the canonical control library to planes per Compliance §2.1.
3. Stand up Drift Detection against substrate-plane signals.
4. Stand up the Evidence plane to the point where provenance-records
   are queryable as a graph.

### 4.2 Activities

| # | Activity | Output |
|---|---|---|
| B1 | SRF L4 (Capture) deployed at every crossing in the boundary registry | `boundary-crossing` records flowing to Evidence |
| B2 | Plane mapping (Compliance §2.1 step 2) executed against the canonical control library | Per-control plane bindings |
| B3 | Signal selection and evidence-pattern declaration (Compliance §2.1 steps 3–4) for each binding | Binding registry |
| B4 | Cadence assignment and arbitration rules (Compliance §2.1 steps 5–6) | Completed binding registry |
| B5 | Gap recording (Compliance §2.1 step 7) for controls without observable signal | Initial POA&M baseline |
| B6 | Drift Detection deployed against substrate-plane signals; drift taxonomy operational | Drift-record stream |
| B7 | Evidence plane provenance graph queryable; OSCAL Component projection regenerable | OSCAL Component artifact |
| B8 | Tier-2 KSI surface stood up against per-binding state | ISSO-tier KSI feed |
| B9 | SRF L1 (Carry) and L2 (Reconstruct) deployed where §3.1 row R cells require it | Per-crossing signal-preservation report |

### 4.3 Conformance Gate (Day 60)

The window closes when Compliance §7 conformance criteria 1–4 are
demonstrably satisfied:

- Every applicable control has a binding or an open POA&M item.
- Every binding satisfies the §2.2 quality bar.
- KSIs are derived per §3.1 with declared tiering and cadence.
- The evidence chain satisfies all six §4.1 properties.

If the gate is not satisfied, Days 61–90 do not begin. Sequencing
principles 2 and 3 are non-negotiable.

---

## 5. Days 61–90 — Conformance & cATO Posture

### 5.1 Objectives

1. Reach authorization-conforming state for the in-scope deployment.
2. Stand up the cATO authorization-state derivation per Compliance §5.1.
3. Establish the AO decision-support surface per Compliance §5.3.
4. Demonstrate 3PAO reproducibility per Compliance §5.4.

### 5.2 Activities

| # | Activity | Output |
|---|---|---|
| C1 | SRF L3 (Re-attest) and L5 (Arbitrate) deployed where §3.1 attributed-degradation cells require it | Compensating-control registry; arbitration ledger |
| C2 | OSCAL SSP, SAR, POA&M, Profile artifacts regenerable from chain on demand | Full OSCAL artifact set |
| C3 | cATO authorization-state derivation rule live; derivation trace exposed | Authorization-state feed |
| C4 | Re-assessment trigger pipeline (Compliance §5.2) wired to all five trigger classes | Trigger-event stream |
| C5 | AO decision-support surface presents the five required elements (Compliance §5.3) | AO surface |
| C6 | 3PAO reproducibility audit conducted against a sample of authorization-state values | Reproducibility audit report |
| C7 | Tier-1 KSI surface stood up against authorization-state | AO-tier KSI feed |
| C8 | Boundary Appendix §6 conformance criteria 5–8 evidenced (boundary-conforming) | Boundary-conformance posture report |
| C9 | POA&M closure rule (Compliance §6.2) operational; closure-by-narrative blocked | POA&M audit |

### 5.3 Conformance Gate (Day 90)

The window closes when **all** of the following are demonstrably true:

- Phase 1 §7 conformance criteria 1–8 satisfied.
- Boundary Appendix §6 conformance criteria 1–8 satisfied.
- Compliance §7 conformance criteria 1–8 satisfied.
- AO has accepted the authorization-state derivation rule and the AO
  surface as decision-support inputs.
- 3PAO has reproduced at least one authorization-state value from the
  evidence chain alone.

This is the program's exit criterion for the 90-day window. It is the
operational expression of sequencing principle 4.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Workstreams

Five workstreams span all three windows. They are not phased; they
operate continuously from Day 1.

| Workstream | Purpose | Cadence |
|---|---|---|
| **Governance** | Canon-change protocol enforcement; ADR review; schema validation; drift scan | Continuous; gated on every artifact change |
| **Evidence Hygiene** | Provenance graph integrity; schema conformance; attribution audit | Continuous; sampled audit weekly |
| **Communications** | Stakeholder updates to CIO/CISO/ISSO/AO/3PAO; status against gates; risk posture | Weekly status; per-gate decision brief |
| **Risk Management** | Active tracking of Phase 0 §7, Boundary §7, Compliance §8 risk registers | Reviewed at every weekly status |
| **Exception Management** | Attributed-degradation thresholds tracked; arbitration-ledger growth monitored | Reviewed at every gate |

Governance and Evidence Hygiene are gating; failures in either pause
the affected window's activities until resolved.

---

## 7. Milestones and Decision Points

| Day | Milestone | Decision Owner | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 7 | Plane-owner attestation complete | Program leadership | Confirm or reassign plane ownership |
| 14 | Boundary discovery scan complete | CISO + ISSO | Accept boundary inventory or extend discovery |
| 30 | Substrate gate (Phase 1 §7 criteria 1–4) | CIO + CISO | Open Days 31–60 or extend Days 1–30 |
| 45 | Binding registry complete | ISSO + Compliance lead | Accept binding set or revise plane mappings |
| 60 | Capture gate (Compliance §7 criteria 1–4) | CISO + AO | Open Days 61–90 or extend Days 31–60 |
| 75 | OSCAL artifact set regenerable | Compliance lead | Confirm OSCAL projection fidelity |
| 90 | Authorization-conforming gate | AO (with 3PAO reproducibility evidence) | Accept cATO posture; close 90-day window |

Decision-owner roles are stated by function, not by individual; the
program's RACI assigns named persons under separate cover.

---

## 8. Risk Posture for the 90 Days

The 90-day window inherits the full risk register from Phase 0 §7,
Boundary §7, and Compliance §8. Three risks are particularly acute
during the 90-day window and are tracked with elevated cadence.

| # | Risk | Window-Specific Trigger | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| E1 | Substrate gate slip cascades into Days 31–60 | Plane-owner attestation incomplete by Day 7 | Escalate to CIO; consider reducing substrate scope, never relaxing gate criteria |
| E2 | SRF L4 capture is partial; some crossings remain unobserved | Boundary registry has crossings without `boundary-crossing` records by Day 45 | Treat the unobserved crossings as gaps; record in POA&M; do not advance to Days 61–90 with hidden boundaries |
| E3 | AO accepts authorization-state without derivation-trace review | AO sign-off requested without §5.3 surface elements 1 and 5 | Compliance plane may not present authorization-state without the derivation trace; this is enforced architecturally |

Risk E3 is the operational expression of "evidence before KSI" and
"conformance before cATO." It is the single risk that, if realized,
invalidates the 90-day program's deliverable.

---

## 9. Exit Criteria and Hand-off

The 90-day program closes when, and only when, the Day-90 conformance
gate (§5.3) is satisfied. At that point:

1. The deployment is **authorization-conforming** as defined by Phase 1
   §7, Boundary §6, and Compliance §7.
2. The cATO authorization-state derivation is operational and accepted
   by the AO.
3. The OSCAL artifact set is regenerable from the canonical evidence
   chain on demand.
4. The 3PAO has reproduced at least one authorization-state value from
   the evidence chain alone.
5. The five cross-cutting workstreams transition from program-mode to
   sustained-operations mode under the same governance.

Post-90-day operations are governed by the cATO model (Compliance §5),
not by a successor execution plan. The program's deliverable is the
sustained operating posture, not the 90-day window itself.

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Document Governance
This 90-Day Execution Plan is a canonical program artifact. Changes are
governed by the canon-change protocol; substantive revisions require an
ADR, a schema check, and a drift scan. The plan derives from the Phase
0, Phase 1, Boundary Modernization Appendix, and Compliance & ATO
Alignment baselines, and is the closing artifact of the master plan
series.
:::
